alerts.title: Iran launches ballistic missiles at Israel in first major attack since ceasefire collapse
alerts.summary: Iran reportedly launched multiple ballistic missiles at Israel following Israeli strikes on Beirut, marking the first significant Iranian retaliation since the fragile US-brokered ceasefire fractured on 2026-06-14 (AP News, incidents feed). This escalation directly contradicts Trump administration warnings and threatens collapse of nascent US-Iran deal negotiations.

alerts.title: Iran 60% enriched; Trump pauses military uranium seizure plan as deal stalls
alerts.summary: Iran maintains 60% U-235 enrichment with ~1-2 week nuclear breakout window. US military had prepared ground mission to secure Iranian enriched uranium but Trump administration paused operation amid deal negotiations (CNN, CBS News, global_metrics nuclear headlines). IAEA board resolution demanded Iran report uranium stocks as verification crisis deepens.

alerts.title: Trump condemns Israeli Beirut strike; US-Iran deal 'very close' but jeopardized
alerts.summary: President Trump explicitly faulted Israel's attack on southern Beirut targeting Hezbollah, stating 'Israeli strike on Beirut shouldn't have happened' and urging stand-down (The Washington Post, PBS, Axios, incidents feed). Trump announced US and Iran 'very close' to deal including oil sanctions waiver and nuclear limits, but Israeli action has placed agreement at risk as Iran threatens retaliation.

alerts.title: Israel prepares for imminent Iranian ballistic missile response; alert level raised
alerts.summary: Israeli defense officials elevated alert posture and activated air defense preparations following Beirut strikes, with IDF leadership warning of imminent Iranian missile attack within hours to days (Jerusalem Post, Times of Israel, Middle East Spectator ~36K views, incidents feed). Multiple Israeli officials confirmed active preparations for incoming ballistic fire.

alerts.title: US sentiment splits evenly on Trump Iran deal amid strike escalation
alerts.summary: US news sentiment on Iran conflict shifted to 47% negative / 47% positive / 6% neutral, with 'deal' and 'peace' competing against 'war,' 'strikes,' and 'attacks' in trending keywords (sentiment feed, 2026-06-14 analysis). Polarized coverage reflects domestic division over Trump's simultaneous pursuit of ceasefire and tacit acceptance of Israeli military escalation.

analysis_note: US-Iran ceasefire framework faces imminent collapse as Israel's Beirut strikes trigger Iranian ballistic missile response, while Trump administration attempts to salvage nuclear deal negotiations amid 60% enrichment threat and 1-2 week breakout window.
